author: @Unpopular_0pinion
text: Oh Robert.. My heart aches for the pain and the emptiness you must've felt. Thank you.. Thank you for your tenatious curiosity, and your generosity with knowledge. You were a damn good man, a great teacher, and I'm guessing an even better husband. I miss the days of being so lucky as to see new videos from you. Getting to check in on what was currently captivating you was a really good thing in my life. I hope you found your peace 💙😞

text: RIP. I enjoyed your videos 😊

author: @mkhamlach
text:
Good life....is it to be happy, or a life without problem, or have what you want..? .In my opinion I don t think people have good or bad life Some people seem to have a lot of probleme and become stronger. And other  have a very good situation but they are .
For me if you good or bad life is like weather.
If it s raining you can sing in the rain. If you want to be happy force yourself to be happy lol.
I know that s really easy to say it. But do something really easy and you will see real changes. Just smile to people....

replies.author: @mkhamlach
replies.text: I forget to say that s a great.message mate.  Last time I drink a coffe with a man and we talked about life money.... He told to me If have 100 euros or nothing in my pocket, no matter because in my heart I am the richest in the world..

author: @Candyman97
text: I would have to say that most people's problems, are they don't like themselves. Or in most cases don't give themselves permission to like themselves! I have always thought that before people can like you, you have to like yourself. The rest of what you talked about will fall in place, once you have the foundation of learning to like you for you, and not what people think you should be.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: Eflat Productions i think that's on it's head mate - we see ourselves in the mirror of others - if we concentrate on liking ourselves then it becomes egocentric - if we concentrate on liking others then that reflects back in the mirror i just mentioned and we end up liking ourselves. I hate to say the next bit - but - liking yourself first was an 80s adage that really lead nowhere

author: @kendalcole7440
text:
Thanks Robert, this was really good.

My notion of a good life is a happy one, and my recipe for happiness is also simple:
- Someone to love.
- Something to do.
- Something to look forward to.

Whenever it gets overwhelming, or when it seems there's no reason, these things bring me back to a good state of mind.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: Kendal Cole nicely put mate

author: @TK42138
text: Interesting subject. I think sometimes the hardest thing is trying not to pre-judge people before even getting to know something about them. One of my fave sayings is 'before judging me, walk a mile in my shoes'. Sometimes previous experiences and environment moulds a person's outlook on life and how they interact with others.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering

author: @actudoran
text:
Haha ... Part of being happy is feeling borderline content with who you are and what defines you as a whole.
Seeing your limitations and thus forming aspirations... 
Admiring other people for their positive attributes without judgment for their apparent flaws ... 
Learning something new each day and going to bed a wiser buddy than the moring...
Surround yourself of  people whom you stand a chance of learning something new.
Never say never
Invest time in kids and inspire rather than dictate
Ask questions no matter how silly and teach kids to ask and think ... Cause effect... 
Forward thinking is a big one for kids and me for it forces imagination to think of a string of events forking possibilities and such
When you hit the sack ... You don't know it but you're happy ... :)

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: actudoran nice addition mate cheers

author: @AshleyMillsTube
text: well said, I think the point about liking others is very important. Liking others is a positive act that helps them to focus on the parts of themselves which they perceive as positive, whereas disliking and judging others is more likely to trigger a sense of rejection in that person which will get them to focus on the negative things they perceive about themselves. People tend to project onto the world what they feel about themselves, so I believe the best thing to do is make people feel good about themselves. Thanks for the video :)

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: Ashley Mills it's about spirals mate when you are on a positive spiral you spiral up - when you are on a negative spiral you spiral down - liking others puts you on a positive spiral

author: @VehicularDIY
text: So simple yet so profound.  Your videos are very inspiring. I feel the most fulfillment when I discover solutions to technical problems.  I've primarily been able to exhibit this skill with automobiles.  My career is focused on uninterruptible power supplies and I hope to one day contribute to their efficiency, your videos always stir the mind.  I just wanted to say thank you.  Of all the channels I've subscribed to on YouTube yours strikes a chord that no other has for me.
